topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the administration and funding of veterans' benefits and military construction.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to alter the information technology system of the Veterans Benefits Administration
Conditions such post-9/11 program eligibility on the individual meeting the requirements for the post-9/11 program as of the date of election.
Document 2: Makes supplemental appropriations for the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) for: (1) general operating expenses; (2) information technology systems; and (3) construction for major projects.

topic.name: Trade Policy Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes related to trade preferences and tariff suspensions for specific products and countries.

Document 1: Amends the Andean Trade Preference Act (ATPA)
Extends preferential treatment to certain wool apparel articles
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
suspension of duty on certain cases or containers
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
suspend temporarily the duty on man-made shells

topic.name: Election and Voting Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at ensuring fairness and transparency in the electoral process, including voting rights and candidate disclosures.

Document 1: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to revise requirements for the treatment of provisional ballots in federal elections.
Prohibits any election recount until all provisional votes have been counted.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require each candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns.
Shall be treated in the same manner as a report filed by the candidate and, except for the appropriate redaction of certain information, shall be made publicly available.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition.
Requires the NLRB to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on specific chemical compounds and yarn types.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on 4-Sulfo-1,8-naphthalic anhydride potassium salt.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on [2,2'-bi-1H-indole]-3,3'-diol, potassium sodium salt.
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on yarn of combed cashmere or yarn of camel hair.

topic.name: Regulatory Measures on Rights and Protections
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at protecting individual rights and ensuring proper regulatory measures in different contexts, including asset seizure, voter integrity, and children's online privacy.

Document 1: revises the authority and procedures that the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) uses to seize property
the IRS may only seize property it suspects has been structured to avoid BSA reporting requirements if the property was derived from an illegal source
Document 2: makes it unlawful for anyone before or during a federal election to knowingly communicate, or attempt to communicate, false election-related information
increases from one year to five years' imprisonment the criminal penalty for intimidation of voters
Document 3: amends the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act of 1998 to apply the prohibitions against collecting personal information from children to online applications and mobile applications
requires verifiable parental consent for the collection, use, or disclosure of personal information of a child

topic.name: Medicaid and Medicare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to Medicaid and Medicare programs focusing on healthcare services, funding restrictions, and patient rights.

Document 1: amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
annual audit of payment claims under a state Medicaid plan
Document 2: Compassionate Assistance for Rape Emergencies Act of 2011
federal funds from being provided to a hospital under title XVIII (Medicare)

topic.name: Healthcare Quality and Access Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to quality healthcare services and technology for specific populations.

Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to review certain quality measures endorsed under the Medicare program that relate to the care of childbearing women and newborns.
Develop and publish a set of maternity care quality measures for the Medicaid and CHIP programs.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on public health preparedness, access to treatments, and regulatory oversight of health-related products.

Document 1: Vaccine Shortage Preparedness Act of 2008
authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to sell to public health departments
Document 2: Early Access to Treatment Act
require a drug that is not approved for marketing to be made available for treatment use

topic.name: Energy Security and Development
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing energy security, promoting renewable energy, and supporting rural development in the context of agricultural and energy policies.

Document 1: Amends the Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to make state grants for extension, education, applied research, and development activities at institutions of higher education, state agencies, or partnerships for local and regional bioenergy and biobased product use and production.
Directs the Secretary to identify barriers to small, beginning, limited resource, and socially disadvantaged producers in conservation and farm support programs.
Document 2: Establishes in the Executive Office of the President a national energy security program to coordinate federal activities and policies to reduce oil consumption by 8 million barrels per day by calendar 2030.
Directs the Secretary of Energy to develop a national plan for electric drive vehicle deployment, including specified goals for deployment of plug-in electric drive vehicles.
Document 3: Prohibits oil and gas preleasing or leasing of any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) that was not available for oil and gas leasing as of July 1, 2008, unless that action is expressly authorized by this Act.

Directs the Secretary of the Interior to ensure that any activity under this subtitle is carried out in a manner that protects the coastal environment, marine environment, and human environment of state coastal zones and the OCS.

topic.name: H-1B Visa Regulations
topic.description: Legislative amendments and regulations concerning the H-1B nonimmigrant visa program and employer responsibilities.

Document 1: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to revise the H-1B nonimmigrant visa (specialty occupation) program
must offer an annual wage to the H-1B nonimmigrant that is the greater of the annual wage paid to the U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident employee
Document 2: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to revise the H-1B nonimmigrant visa (specialty occupation) program
must offer an annual wage to the H-1B nonimmigrant that is the greater of the annual wage paid to the U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident employee

topic.name: Veterans and Workforce Development
topic.description: Legislative acts aimed at supporting veterans and enhancing workforce training programs.

Document 1: Amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1998
require a one-stop delivery system, in selecting and approving training services
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Associate Administrator for Veterans Business Development
establish a Women Veterans Business Training Resource Program
Document 3: authorizes appropriations for the Coast Guard
develop and implement a plan to conduct every two years a multirater assessment

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at enhancing national security through sanctions, investment regulations, and detainee management.

Document 1: U.S. sanctions, controls, and regulations relating to Syria shall remain in effect until the President certifies that Syria has ceased support for terrorism
Imposes specified trade, assistance, and military sanctions, as appropriate, on persons or countries that transfer goods or technology
Document 2: Permits a foreign person owned, in whole or in part, or controlled by a foreign government to acquire ownership in either a business, or in real property in the United States
Subjects investment and operation of critical infrastructure in the United States by such a foreign person to the same reciprocity requirements
Document 3: Prohibits the obligation or expenditure of funds to transfer or release any covered detainee at the U.S. Naval Station Guantanamo Bay
Makes a U.S. officer or employee liable in his or her individual capacity for a civil penalty of $10,000 for each covered detainee transferred or released in violation of such prohibition

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Employment and Savings
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures that provide tax benefits to promote employment and enhance retirement savings for individuals.

Document 1: retained worker tax credit
employ an average of fewer than 100 employees
Document 2: expand eligibility for the retirement savings tax credit
increasing income eligibility limits
Document 3: expand the exceptions that permit penalty-free distributions
penalty-free distributions to unemployed individuals from retirement plans

topic.name: Legislative Changes and Protections
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative amendments and acts aimed at altering membership criteria, observance dates, and civilian protection regulations.

1: Amends the Ysleta del Sur Pueblo and Alabama and Coushatta Indian Tribes of Texas Restoration Act to remove the blood quantum requirement for determining the membership of descendants of members of the Ysleta del Sur Pueblo Indian Tribe.
2: Changes the date of the observance of the birthday of President George Washington to February 22 of each year (currently, the third Monday in February).
3: Places specified restrictions on the use of cluster munitions.

Directs the President, within 90 days after the use of such munitions, to submit to the congressional defense, appropriations, and foreign relations committees a plan for cleaning up any such munitions or submunitions which fail to explode and continue to pose a hazard to civilians.

topic.name: Federal Initiatives for Health and Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal legislative efforts aimed at improving health and educational outcomes through research, training, and resource allocation.

Document 1: Federal Response to Eliminate Eating Disorders Act of 2009 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the National Institutes of Health to take certain actions regarding eating disorder research.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide for the collection, analysis, and reporting of epidemiological data on eating disorders.
Document 2: Amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to direct the Administrator of FEMA to establish the Railroad Emergency Services Preparedness Subcommittee.
Directs the Subcommittee to develop recommendations for improving emergency responder training and resource allocation.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative efforts to promote renewable energy, enhance energy efficiency, and reduce greenhouse gas emissions in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978
require the percentage of electric energy generated from renewable sources to increase each year
Document 2: developing alternative technology vehicles
developing and building energy efficient buildings that use no more than 50% of the energy
Document 3: allow a tax credit for up to $500 of the cost of replacing wood stoves with energy efficiency standards
extend through 2011 the tax credit for producing electricity from certain renewable resources

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at optimizing financial assistance and regulatory frameworks for borrowers and small businesses.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act
establish rules that lenders must follow regarding the overpayment and underpayment of student loan payments
Document 2: direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make financial assistance available to franchisees
a franchisee shall not be considered affiliated with the franchisor
Document 3: amends the Banking Act of 1933 (Glass-Steagall Act) to prohibit a member bank from being affiliated with specified organizations
prohibits a depository institution from engaging in the business of insurance

topic.name: Land Management and Rights
topic.description: The documents discuss various laws and acts related to the management, sale, and rights associated with federal and tribal lands in the United States.

Document 1: Repeals the Act of May 31, 1918
Gives the Shoshone-Bannock Tribes of the Fort Hall Indian Reservation the exclusive right of first refusal to purchase land
Document 2: Authorizes the Departments of the Interior and Agriculture to offer for competitive sale by auction federal land
Requires proceeds to be deposited into the Highway Trust Fund
Document 3: Authorizes a state to petition the Secretary of the Interior for state management of federal land
Sets forth requirements for the approval or denial of petitions

topic.name: Education and Health Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving health services and safety in educational settings, particularly for under-resourced schools and vulnerable student populations.

Document 1: Nurses for Under-Resourced Schools Everywhere Act
grant priority to high-need LEAs
Document 2: Campus Safety for Children Act of 2012
statistics on sexual offenses against minors
Document 3: Expanding School Choice Act
allocate school improvement grant funds based on family income

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts aimed at reforming and regulating key sectors such as child support enforcement, housing finance, and terrorism insurance.

Document 1: Strengthen and Vitalize Enforcement of Child Support (SAVE Child Support) Act
Amends part D (Child Support and Establishment of Paternity) of title IV of the Social Security Act
Document 2: Housing Finance Reform and Taxpayer Protection Act of 2014
Elimination of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac
Document 3: Terrorism Risk Insurance Program Reauthorization Act of 2014
Extension of Terrorism Insurance Program

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Safety
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and public welfare.

Document 1: Saracini Aviation Safety Act of 2015
installation of secondary barriers to prevent access to the flight deck
Document 2: amends the Safe Drinking Water Act
grant program to help schools and day cares in replacing service lines and solder that are not lead free
Document 3: designates a postal facility
W. Ronald Coale Memorial Post Office Building

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies related to the benefits and educational assistance available to veterans and service members.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to make a decision on each benefits claim
establish a task force to monitor the process by which benefits claims are decided
Document 2: Montgomery GI Bill Educational Assistance Transferability Act
authorize the Secretary of the military department concerned to permit any member of the Armed Forces to transfer a portion of such assistance
Document 3: Fulfilling Our Promise to Student Veterans Act
authorize members and former members of the Armed Forces to request the correction of erroneous Army College Fund benefit amounts

topic.name: Consumer and Civil Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative measures aimed at protecting consumer rights and defining civil liabilities in specific contexts.

Document 1: Call Center Consumer's Right to Know Act
requires each call center employee to disclose the physical location
Document 2: Every Child is a Blessing Act of 2014
Prohibits recovery of damages in certain civil actions based on a claim that a child would not or should not have been born.
Document 3: Citizens' Self-Defense Act of 2007
a person not prohibited under the Brady Handgun Violence Prevention Act from receiving a firearm shall have the right to obtain firearms for security

topic.name: Water and Environmental Management
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to the management and restoration of water resources and ecosystems in specific regions of the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to plan, design, and construct a regional water system
to divert water to the Pueblos and to the County Water Utility
Document 2: The bill amends the findings and purposes of the Lake Tahoe Restoration Act
require the Forest Service to coordinate with the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and state, local, and private entities
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to establish a program to provide loans and loan guarantees
requires a complementary plan for sustainable infrastructure development that provides for the mitigation of environmental impacts

topic.name: Agricultural Tax Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax benefits and incentives for agricultural activities and conservation efforts.

Document 1: American Family Farm and Ranchland Protection Act
increase the estate tax exclusion for land subject to a qualified conservation easement
Document 2: qualify Indian tribes as eligible recipients of tax deductible contributions
Bill Emerson Good Samaritan Food Donation Act
Document 3: allow the special depreciation allowance for dairy producing calves and cows
bonus depreciation for livestock purchased for business use
